question:
1. Is the infinite series
\[
\sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^{(n+1) / n}}
\]
convergent? Prove your statement.
solution:
Solution. For every positive integer \( n, n<2^{\prime \prime} \). Hence \( n^{1 "}<2 \), so
\[
\frac{1}{n^{(n+1) n}}>\frac{1}{2 n} .
\]

Since \( \sum_{n}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2 n} \) diverges, so does \( \sum_{n}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^{(n+1) n}} \).

descriptive_long.question:
1. Is the infinite series
\[
\sum_{indexer=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{indexer^{(indexer+1) / indexer}}
\]
convergent? Prove your statement.
descriptive_long.solution:
Solution. For every positive integer \( indexer, indexer<2^{\prime \prime} \). Hence \( indexer^{1 \"}<2 \), so
\[
\frac{1}{indexer^{(indexer+1) indexer}}>\frac{1}{2 indexer} .
\]

Since \( \sum_{indexer}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2 indexer} \) diverges, so does \( \sum_{indexer}^{\infty} \frac{1}{indexer^{(indexer+1) indexer}} \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
1. Is the infinite series
\[
\sum_{butterfly=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{butterfly^{(butterfly+1) / butterfly}}
\]
convergent? Prove your statement.
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
Solution. For every positive integer \( butterfly, butterfly<2^{\prime \prime} \). Hence \( butterfly^{1 \"}<2 \), so
\[
\frac{1}{butterfly^{(butterfly+1) butterfly}}>\frac{1}{2 butterfly} .
\]

Since \( \sum_{butterfly}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2 butterfly} \) diverges, so does \( \sum_{butterfly}^{\infty} \frac{1}{butterfly^{(butterfly+1) butterfly}} \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
1. Is the infinite series
\[
\sum_{unnatural=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\unnatural^{(\unnatural+1) / \unnatural}}
\]
convergent? Prove your statement.
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Solution. For every positive integer \( \unnatural, \unnatural<2^{\prime \prime} \). Hence \( \unnatural^{1 "}<2 \), so
\[
\frac{1}{\unnatural^{(\unnatural+1) \unnatural}}>\frac{1}{2 \unnatural} .
\]

Since \( \sum_{\unnatural}^{\infty} \frac{1}{2 \unnatural} \) diverges, so does \( \sum_{\unnatural}^{\infty} \frac{1}{\unnatural^{(\unnatural+1) \unnatural}} \).

kernel_variant.solution:
Solution.  Since |f(n)| \leq  n^{1/2}, we have n^{|f(n)|/n} \leq  n^{1/\sqrt{n}} < 4.  Consequently,  
1/n^{(n+f(n))/n} \geq  1/(4n).  Therefore, by the Comparison Test, the series diverges.

_replacement_note.reason: Original kernel variant was too easy compared to the original problem
